summary: Virginia has stronger statewide AI-in-education evidence than the current record showed. VDOE's Technology in Education page documents statewide generative-AI teacher training, a Year of Learning leadership initiative, and an AI Advisory Committee, while the Virginia Board of Education's technology plan directs guidance for integrating AI with expectations for academic integrity.

candidate_sources.title: Technology in Education | Virginia Department of Education
candidate_sources.why_it_matters: This official VDOE page documents statewide teacher training, leadership support, and AI governance activity.

candidate_sources.title: Virginia Education Technology Plan 2024-2030 | Virginia Board of Education
candidate_sources.why_it_matters: This statewide plan explicitly calls for guidance that allows AI integration and sets expectations for academic integrity.

candidate_sources.title: Digital Learning Integration Standards of Learning | Virginia Department of Education
candidate_sources.why_it_matters: This provides standards context but not the clearest field-level AI evidence compared with the two selected sources.

ai_use_allowed.rationale: Virginia has statewide guidance aimed at allowing AI integration with guardrails around academic integrity rather than a blanket prohibition or unrestricted use.
ai_use_allowed.evidence_quote: Develop and recommend guidance that allows for the integration of artificial intelligence and holds expectations for academic integrity.

assessment_policy.rationale: The reviewed statewide sources did not provide sufficiently explicit AI-specific assessment or disclosure rules to support positive coding.

privacy_policy.rationale: The reviewed statewide sources did not include direct AI-specific student-data privacy or procurement controls.

teacher_pd_support.rationale: Virginia is actively backing statewide educator support through workshops and a leadership initiative focused on generative AI integration.
teacher_pd_support.evidence_quote: The Virginia Department of Education (VDOE) provided Generative AI teacher training in the fall of 2024... these workshops enrolled 360 educators.

implementation_stage.rationale: Virginia has moved beyond exploration into active guidance and implementation support through statewide workshops, an AI Advisory Committee, and leadership programming.
implementation_stage.evidence_quote: The VDOE’s AI Advisory Committee provides strategic recommendations, elevates concerns about AI integration, and advises on effectively integrating AI while supporting innovation and responsible digital practices.

open_questions: If Virginia later publishes formal privacy or procurement guidance tied directly to AI systems, privacy coding should be revisited.
Assessment-specific AI guidance may exist in separate superintendent memos or local policy resources and should be monitored.

approval_recommendation.rationale: Virginia now has strong official evidence for AI use guidance, educator support, and active implementation, but the evidence is still stronger on training and governance than on assessment/privacy, making sample audit the right route.

recommended_record.notes: Deep-research review found stronger official statewide evidence for Virginia than the prior placeholder record. VDOE and Board of Education materials support guided AI integration, statewide educator training, and active implementation support.
recommended_record.verification_notes: Official Virginia sources support moderate AI-use guidance and strong teacher-support / implementation coding. Assessment and privacy remain uncoded because the reviewed sources are not explicit on those domains.

source_documents.title: Technology in Education | Virginia Department of Education
Virginia Education Technology Plan 2024-2030 | Virginia Board of Education

evidence_spans.quote: Develop and recommend guidance that allows for the integration of artificial intelligence and holds expectations for academic integrity.
The Virginia Department of Education (VDOE) provided Generative AI teacher training in the fall of 2024... these workshops enrolled 360 educators.

The VDOE’s AI Advisory Committee provides strategic recommendations, elevates concerns about AI integration, and advises on effectively integrating AI while supporting innovation and responsible digital practices.

gold_record.notes: Deep-research-reviewed hard case. Official Virginia sources support guided AI integration and strong statewide educator-support activity.
